task_list.name: Convert Lif Plate to OME-Zarr
task_list.docs_info:
### Purpose

- Convert a Leica LIF plate to a OME-Zarr Plate.

### Outputs

- A OME-Zarr Plate.

### Limitations

- This task has been tested on a limited set of acquisitions. It may not work on all Leica LIF acquisitions.
- This converter is does not support images exported in auto-saved mode.

### Expected inputs

The following plate layout are supported:

* Single Position Plates

    ```text
    /{Project.lif}
    ----/{Tilescan 1}/
    --------/A
    ------------/1 (Simple Image)
    ```

    ```text
    /{Project.lif}
    ----/{Tilescan 1}/
    --------/A1 (Simple Image)
    --------/...
    ```

* Multi Position Plates

    ```text
    /{Project.lif}
    ----/{Tilescan 1}/
    --------/A
    ------------/1
    ----------------/R1 (Simple Image)
    ----------------/R2 (Simple Image)
    ----------------/...
    ```

    ```text
    /{Project.lif}
    ----/{Tilescan 1}/
    --------/A1
    ------------/R1 (Simple Image)
    ------------/R2 (Simple Image)
    ------------/...
    ```

* Mosaique Plates

    ```text
    /{Project.lif}
    ----/{Tilescan 1}/
    --------/A
    ------------/1 (Mosaic Image)
    ------------/...
    ```

    ```text
    /{Project.lif}
    ----/{Tilescan 1}/
    --------/A1 (Mosaic Image)
    --------/...
    ```

The names in curly braces `{}` can be freely chosen by the user. 

While the other names must follow the following format:

* The well name must be a single or double letter followed by a positive integer.
Valid examples are `A1`, `A2`, `B1`, `AA1`, `AA12` etc.
* Alternatively, the well can be hierarchically structured, for example `A/1`, `A/2`, `B/1`, `AA/1`, `AA/12` etc.
* If the well is a multi-position well, the positions must be named `R` followed by a positive integer. Valid examples are `R1`, `R2`, `R3`, `R12` etc.
* In case of more complex plate formats, for example FLIM  data, the converter will ignore the data that does not follow the above formats. For example:

  ```text
  /{Project.lif}
  ----/{Tilescan 1}/
  --------/A/1/R1 (Converted)
  --------/A/1/R1/FLIM/Intensity (Ignored)
  --------------------/Fast Flim (Ignored)
  --------------------/Standard Deviation (Ignored)
  ```

AcquisitionFilter.description: Keep or remove tiles based on their acquisition index (plates only).

mode.description: Whether matching tiles are **included** (kept) or **excluded** (removed).
mode.title: Mode

acquisitions.description:
List of acquisition indices to keep (`Include` mode) or remove
(`Exclude` mode), e.g. `[0, 1]`.
acquisitions.title: Acquisitions

AcquisitionFilter.title: Acquisition Filter

AttributeFilter.description: Keep or remove tiles based on the value of one of their attributes.

mode.description: Whether matching tiles are **included** (kept) or **excluded** (removed).
mode.title: Mode

key.description: Attribute key to match. The attribute must be present on every tile.
key.title: Key

values.description:
Values to match against. A tile matches if *any* element of its
attribute value matches one of these; matching tiles are kept in
`Include` mode and removed in `Exclude` mode.
values.title: Values

mode.description:
Automatically use `Snap to Grid` when the field of view positions
align to a regular grid, `Snap to Corners` otherwise.
mode.title: Mode

tolerance.description:
Maximum misalignment (in pixels) tolerated when checking whether the
field of view positions fit a regular grid.

ChannelFilter.description: Keep or remove tiles based on their channel labels.

mode.description: Whether matching tiles are **included** (kept) or **excluded** (removed).
mode.title: Mode

channel_labels.description:
Channel labels to keep (`Include` mode) or remove (`Exclude` mode),
e.g. `["DAPI", "GFP"]`. A tile matches only if *all* of its channels are
in this list; a partial match **fails the conversion**.
channel_labels.title: Channel Labels

ChannelFilter.title: Channel Filter

ChannelInfoUI.description: Set the name, wavelength, and display color of a channel.

channel_label.description: Name of the channel, e.g. `DAPI` or `GFP`.
channel_label.title: Channel Label

wavelength_id.description:
The wavelength ID of the channel.
Some tasks can use it instead of the channel name, e.g. to apply
illumination correction per wavelength in multiplexed acquisitions.
wavelength_id.title: Wavelength ID

color.description: Display color of the channel, e.g. for visualization purposes.
color.title: Color

ChannelInfoUI.title: ChannelInfoUI

ColorMenu.description: Display color of the channel. `Auto` picks a color based on the channel name or wavelength.
ColorMenu.title: ColorMenu

ConverterOptions.description: Options for the OME-Zarr conversion process.

writer_mode.description:
Mode for writing data during conversion.

- `By Tile`: Write data one tile at a time. This consumes less memory, but may be
  slower.
- `By Tile (Using Dask)`: Write tiles in parallel using Dask. This is
  usually faster than writing by tile sequentially, but may consume more
  memory.
- `By FOV`: Write data one field of view at a time. Often the best compromise
  between speed and memory usage in most cases.
- `By FOV (Using Dask)`: Write fields of view in parallel using Dask.
  This is usually faster than writing by FOV sequentially, but may consume
  more memory.
- `In Memory`: Load all data into memory before writing.
writer_mode.title: Writer Mode

grouping.description:
How fields of view are grouped into output images.

- `Mosaic`: Aggregate all fields of view of an acquisition into one OME-Zarr,
  arranged by the nested `tiling_strategy`.
- `Per-FOV`: Write each field of view as its own OME-Zarr image (no mosaic,
  no tiling strategy).
grouping.title: Grouping

runtime_settings.description:
Advanced performance settings: parallelism, storage codec, and
temporary storage. The defaults are safe for most conversions.
runtime_settings.title: Runtime Settings

ConverterOptions.title: ConverterOptions

DataTypeEnum.description: Pixel data type of the output image; `autodetect` infers it.
DataTypeEnum.title: DataTypeEnum

mode.description: How the conversion work is parallelized.
mode.title: Mode

FloatValue.description: Match attribute elements equal to a decimal value.

FovBasedChunking.description: Store the image in chunks sized like the fields of view.

mode.description: How the image is split into storage chunks.
mode.title: Mode

xy_scaling.description:
Scaling factor for the XY chunk size, relative to the field of view size.

- `1`: chunks match the field of view size.
- `0.5`: chunks are half the field of view (smaller chunks, more files).
- `2`: chunks are double the field of view (larger chunks, fewer files).
xy_scaling.title: XY Scaling Factor

FovBasedChunking.title: Same as FOV

FovNameFilter.description: Keep or remove tiles based on their field of view name.

mode.description: Whether matching tiles are **included** (kept) or **excluded** (removed).
mode.title: Mode

regex.description:
Regex pattern matched against the tile's `fov_name`.

- `Include`: matching tiles are kept, all others removed.
- `Exclude`: matching tiles are removed, all others kept.
regex.title: Regex

FovNameFilter.title: FOV Name Filter

InplaceTiling.description: Keep every field of view at its original stage position.

mode.description:
Keep every field of view at its original stage position. Imprecise
stage positions **may produce artifacts**: where tiles overlap, the last
written tile wins.
mode.title: Mode

LifAcquisitionOptions.description: Acquisition options specific to LIF conversion.

channels.description:
Names, wavelengths, and display colors of the channels. If left
empty, the channel information parsed from the raw metadata is used.
channels.title: Channels

pixel_info.description:
Override the pixel size and the Z/time spacing of the images. If left
empty, the values parsed from the raw metadata are used.
pixel_info.title: Pixel Size Information

condition_table_path.description:
Optional path to a condition table CSV file to store in the plate
metadata.
condition_table_path.title: Condition Table Path

axes.description:
Axes of the image data, e.g. `czyx`. If left empty, the axes parsed
from the raw metadata are used.
axes.title: Axes

data_type.description:
Pixel data type of the output image. `autodetect` infers it from the
input images.
data_type.title: Data Type

stage_orientation.description:
Corrections for the orientation of the microscope stage relative to
the image axes.
stage_orientation.title: Stage Orientation

filters.description: Filters selecting which tiles of the acquisition are converted.
filters.title: Filters

position_scale.description:
Scale factor (m/px) overriding ``lif_image.scale_n[10]``. Set when stage
coordinates need a non-default unit conversion.
position_scale.title: Position Scale

LifAcquisitionOptions.title: LifAcquisitionOptions

LifPlateAcquisitionModel.description: Acquisition input model for LIF plate conversion.

path.description: Path to the acquisition LIF file or directory containing the LIF file(s).
path.title: Path

plate_name.description:
Optional custom name for the plate. If not provided, the name will be the
acquisition directory name.
plate_name.title: Plate Name

acquisition_id.description: Acquisition ID, used to identify the acquisition in case of multiple acquisitions.
acquisition_id.title: Acquisition Id

tile_scan_name.description:
Optional name of the tile scan. If ``None``, all plate-shaped tile scans
in the LIF file are processed (wildcard mode).
tile_scan_name.title: Tile Scan Name

LifPlateAcquisitionModel.title: LifPlateAcquisitionModel

MosaicGrouping.description: Aggregate all fields of view of an acquisition into one mosaic OME-Zarr.

mode.description: How fields of view are grouped into output images.
mode.title: Mode

tiling_strategy.description:
How the fields of view are arranged within the mosaic.

- `Auto`: Automatically determine if `Snap to Grid` is possible,
  otherwise use `Snap to Corners`. Accepts an optional tolerance
  (in pixels) for grid alignment.
- `Snap to Grid`: Tile images to fit a regular grid. This is only
  possible if image positions align to a grid (potentially with
  overlap). Accepts an optional tolerance (in pixels).
- `Snap to Corners`: Tile images to fit a grid defined by the corner
  positions.
- `Inplace`: Write tiles in their original positions without tiling.
  This may lead to artifacts if microscope stage positions are not
  precise.
tiling_strategy.title: Tiling Strategy

MosaicGrouping.title: Mosaic

mode.description: How the resolution levels of the pyramid are defined.
mode.title: Mode

level_names.description:
Names of the resolution levels, from highest to lowest resolution,
e.g. `["s0", "s1", "s2"]`. Each name becomes a path inside the OME-Zarr.
level_names.title: Level Names

NamedLevels.title: Custom Names

mode.description: How the resolution levels of the pyramid are defined.
mode.title: Mode

num_levels.description: Number of resolution levels in the pyramid of the output image.
num_levels.title: Number of Resolution Levels

NumberOfLevels.title: Number of Levels

OmeZarrOptions.description: Options controlling the layout of the output OME-Zarr.

levels.description:
How many resolution levels the output pyramid has and how they are named.

- `Number of Levels`: a number of levels with the default names
  (`0`, `1`, ...).
- `Custom Names`: an explicit list of level names, e.g. `["s0", "s1"]`.
levels.title: Resolution Levels

chunks.description:
How the image is split into storage chunks: sized like the fields of
view (`Same as FOV`) or with fixed sizes (`Fixed Size`).
chunks.title: Chunking Strategy

OME-Zarr.

mode.description: How fields of view are grouped into output images.
mode.title: Mode

Regex pattern matched against the tile's base path.

- `Include`: matching tiles are kept, all others removed.
- `Exclude`: matching tiles are removed, all others kept.

RuntimeSettings.description: Advanced performance settings; the defaults suit most conversions.

use_zarrs_codec.description:
Read and write image data with the `zarrs` Rust backend, which is
usually faster. **Requires** the optional `zarrs` dependency to be
installed.
use_zarrs_codec.title: Use Zarrs Codec Pipeline

dask_scheduler.description:
How the conversion work is parallelized.

- `Threads`: parallelize using multiple threads.
- `Processes`: parallelize using multiple processes.
- `Synchronous`: run sequentially, without parallelism.
- `Default`: keep the environment's parallelism settings unchanged.
dask_scheduler.title: Dask Scheduler

temp_json_options.description: Where and when intermediate conversion data is stored on disk.
temp_json_options.title: Temporary JSON Options

RuntimeSettings.title: RuntimeSettings

Scalings.description: Scaling factor applied to the field of view size to get the chunk size.
Scalings.title: Scalings

SnapToCornersTiling.description: Arrange the fields of view on a grid defined by their corners.

mode.description:
Arrange the fields of view on a grid defined by their corner
positions.
mode.title: Mode

SnapToCornersTiling.title: Snap to Corners

SnapToGridTiling.description: Arrange the fields of view on a regular grid.

mode.description:
Arrange the fields of view on a regular grid. Only possible when
their positions already align to a grid (potentially with overlap).
mode.title: Mode

tolerance.description:
Maximum misalignment (in pixels) tolerated when checking whether the
field of view positions fit a regular grid.
tolerance.title: Tiling Tolerance (in pixels)

SnapToGridTiling.title: Snap to Grid

StageOrientation.description: Corrections for the stage orientation relative to the image axes.

flip_x.description: Whether to flip the position along the X axis.
flip_x.title: Flip X

flip_y.description: Whether to flip the position along the Y axis.
flip_y.title: Flip Y

swap_xy.description: Whether to swap the positions along the X and Y axes.
swap_xy.title: Swap XY

StageOrientation.title: StageOrientation

StagePositionCorrections.description: Corrections applied to the stage positions before writing the image.

remove_xy_offset.description:
Whether to shift the image so its XY origin is 0.

- `Global`: Shift all positions together so the image origin is 0.
- `Keep`: Use the stage positions as-is. **Fails** if any position is
  negative; positive positions produce empty padding at the image origin.
remove_xy_offset.title: Remove XY Offset

remove_z_offset.description:
Whether to shift the image so its Z origin is 0.

- `Global`: Shift all positions together so the Z origin is 0.
- `Per-FOV`: Shift each field of view independently to Z origin 0.
- `Keep`: Use the stage positions as-is. **Fails** if any position is
  negative; positive positions produce empty padding at the image origin.
remove_z_offset.title: Remove Z Offset

remove_t_offset.description:
Whether to shift the image so its time origin is 0.

- `Global`: Shift all positions together so the time origin is 0.
- `Keep`: Use the stage positions as-is. **Fails** if any position is
  negative; positive positions produce empty padding at the image origin.
remove_t_offset.title: Remove T Offset

remove_xy_jitter.description:
Remove small stage position inconsistencies within a field of view by
snapping its sub-tiles to a shared origin.
remove_xy_jitter.title: Remove XY Jitter

reindex_channels.description:
If enabled, only the channels actually present are converted; if
disabled, missing channels are stored as empty images.
reindex_channels.title: Reindex Channels

StagePositionCorrections.title: StagePositionCorrections

StringValue.description: Match attribute elements equal to a text value.

type.description: Type of the value to match.
type.title: Type

StringValue.title: String Value

SynchronousScheduler.description: Run the conversion sequentially, without parallelism.

mode.description: How the conversion work is parallelized.
mode.title: Mode

SynchronousScheduler.title: Synchronous

TRangeFilter.description: Keep only tiles whose starting time point lies within a range.

min_t.description:
Minimum starting time point (inclusive). Leave empty for no lower
bound. Tiles are kept or dropped **whole**, judged by their starting time
point only.
min_t.title: Min T

max_t.description:
Maximum starting time point (inclusive). Leave empty for no upper
bound. Tiles are kept or dropped **whole**, judged by their starting time
point only.
max_t.title: Max T

TRangeFilter.title: Time Range Filter

TempJsonOptions.description: Where and when intermediate conversion data is stored on disk.

temp_url.description:
Where intermediate JSON files are written. The `{zarr_dir}`
placeholder is replaced by the task's output directory.
temp_url.title: Temporary Storage URL

serialization.description:
Serialization mode for tiled image data between init and compute phases.

- `Memory`: always keep data in-memory (skips all filesystem I/O).
- `JSON`: always write to a temporary JSON file on disk (**required** for
  distributed Fractal runs where init and compute execute on different
  machines).
- `Auto`: use in-memory when the total serialized payload is within
  `max_in_memory_bytes` (default 10 MiB), otherwise fall back to JSON files
  on disk.
serialization.title: Serialization

max_in_memory_bytes.description:
Maximum total size of serialized tiled image data to keep in-memory
between init and compute phases when `serialization` is `Auto`.
If the total size exceeds this threshold, data will be written to temporary
JSON files on disk instead. Default is 10 MiB.
max_in_memory_bytes.title: Max In-Memory Bytes

TempJsonOptions.title: TempJsonOptions

mode.description: How the conversion work is parallelized.
mode.title: Mode

num_workers.description: Number of worker threads to use. Must be at least 1.
num_workers.title: Number of Threads

ThreadScheduler.title: Threads

ConverterOptions.description: Options for the OME-Zarr conversion process.

writer_mode.description:
Mode for writing data during conversion.

- `By Tile`: Write data one tile at a time. This consumes less memory, but may be
  slower.
- `By Tile (Using Dask)`: Write tiles in parallel using Dask. This is
  usually faster than writing by tile sequentially, but may consume more
  memory.
- `By FOV`: Write data one field of view at a time. Often the best compromise
  between speed and memory usage in most cases.
- `By FOV (Using Dask)`: Write fields of view in parallel using Dask.
  This is usually faster than writing by FOV sequentially, but may consume
  more memory.
- `In Memory`: Load all data into memory before writing.
writer_mode.title: Writer Mode

grouping.description:
How fields of view are grouped into output images.

- `Mosaic`: Aggregate all fields of view of an acquisition into one OME-Zarr,
  arranged by the nested `tiling_strategy`.
- `Per-FOV`: Write each field of view as its own OME-Zarr image (no mosaic,
  no tiling strategy).
grouping.title: Grouping

Advanced performance settings: parallelism, storage codec, and
temporary storage. The defaults are safe for most conversions.
runtime_settings.title: Runtime Settings

- `Auto`: Automatically determine if `Snap to Grid` is possible,

How the conversion work is parallelized.

- `Threads`: parallelize using multiple threads.
- `Processes`: parallelize using multiple processes.
- `Synchronous`: run sequentially, without parallelism.
- `Default`: keep the environment's parallelism settings unchanged.

StagePositionCorrections.description: Corrections applied to the stage positions before writing the image.

remove_xy_offset.description:
Whether to shift the image so its XY origin is 0.

- `Global`: Shift all positions together so the image origin is 0.
- `Keep`: Use the stage positions as-is. **Fails** if any position is
  negative; positive positions produce empty padding at the image origin.
remove_xy_offset.title: Remove XY Offset

remove_z_offset.description:
Whether to shift the image so its Z origin is 0.

- `Global`: Shift all positions together so the Z origin is 0.
- `Per-FOV`: Shift each field of view independently to Z origin 0.
- `Keep`: Use the stage positions as-is. **Fails** if any position is
  negative; positive positions produce empty padding at the image origin.
remove_z_offset.title: Remove Z Offset

remove_t_offset.description:
Whether to shift the image so its time origin is 0.

- `Global`: Shift all positions together so the time origin is 0.
- `Keep`: Use the stage positions as-is. **Fails** if any position is
  negative; positive positions produce empty padding at the image origin.
remove_t_offset.title: Remove T Offset

remove_xy_jitter.description:
Remove small stage position inconsistencies within a field of view by
snapping its sub-tiles to a shared origin.
remove_xy_jitter.title: Remove XY Jitter

reindex_channels.description:
If enabled, only the channels actually present are converted; if
disabled, missing channels are stored as empty images.
reindex_channels.title: Reindex Channels

Where intermediate JSON files are written. The `{zarr_dir}`
placeholder is replaced by the task's output directory.
temp_url.title: Temporary Storage URL

serialization.description:
Serialization mode for tiled image data between init and compute phases.

- `Memory`: always keep data in-memory (skips all filesystem I/O).
- `JSON`: always write to a temporary JSON file on disk (**required** for
  distributed Fractal runs where init and compute execute on different
  machines).
- `Auto`: use in-memory when the total serialized payload is within
  `max_in_memory_bytes` (default 10 MiB), otherwise fall back to JSON files
  on disk.
serialization.title: Serialization

max_in_memory_bytes.description:
Maximum total size of serialized tiled image data to keep in-memory
between init and compute phases when `serialization` is `Auto`.
If the total size exceeds this threshold, data will be written to temporary
JSON files on disk instead. Default is 10 MiB.
max_in_memory_bytes.title: Max In-Memory Bytes

TempJsonOptions.title: TempJsonOptions

task_list.name: Convert Lif Image to OME-Zarr
task_list.docs_info:
### Purpose

- Convert a Leica LIF acquisition to a OME-Zarr Image.

### Outputs

- A OME-Zarr Image.

### Limitations

- This task has been tested on a limited set of acquisitions. It may not work on all Leica LIF acquisitions.
- This converter is does not support images exported in auto-saved mode.

### Expected inputs

The following image layout are supported:

* Single Position Image

    ```text
    /{Project.lif}
    ----/{Tilescan 1} (Simple Image)
    ```

* Multi Position Image
  
    ```text
    /{Project.lif}
    ----/{Tilescan 1}/
    --------/Position 1 (Simple Image)
    --------/Position 2 (Simple Image)
    --------/...
    ```

* Mosaique Image
  
    ```text
    /{Project.lif}
    ----/{Tilescan 1} (Mosaic Image)
    ```


The names in curly braces `{}` can be freely chosen by the user. While
the other names must follow the following format:

If the image is a multi-position image, the positions must be named `Position` followed by a space and a positive integer. Valid examples are `Position 1`, `Position 2`, `Position 3`, `Position 12` etc.

Moreover, if the lif file contains scans that do not follow the above formats, the converter will ignore them.

mode.title: Mode

acquisitions.description:
List of acquisition indices to keep (`Include` mode) or remove
(`Exclude` mode), e.g. `[0, 1]`.

Values to match against. A tile matches if *any* element of its
attribute value matches one of these; matching tiles are kept in
`Include` mode and removed in `Exclude` mode.

Maximum misalignment (in pixels) tolerated when checking whether the

The wavelength ID of the channel.
Some tasks can use it instead of the channel name, e.g. to apply

ConverterOptions.description: Options for the OME-Zarr conversion process.

writer_mode.description:
Mode for writing data during conversion.

- `By Tile`: Write data one tile at a time. This consumes less memory, but may be
  slower.
- `By Tile (Using Dask)`: Write tiles in parallel using Dask. This is
  usually faster than writing by tile sequentially, but may consume more
  memory.
- `By FOV`: Write data one field of view at a time. Often the best compromise
  between speed and memory usage in most cases.
- `By FOV (Using Dask)`: Write fields of view in parallel using Dask.
  This is usually faster than writing by FOV sequentially, but may consume
  more memory.
- `In Memory`: Load all data into memory before writing.
writer_mode.title: Writer Mode

grouping.description:
How fields of view are grouped into output images.

- `Mosaic`: Aggregate all fields of view of an acquisition into one OME-Zarr,
  arranged by the nested `tiling_strategy`.
- `Per-FOV`: Write each field of view as its own OME-Zarr image (no mosaic,
  no tiling strategy).
grouping.title: Grouping

runtime_settings.description:
Advanced performance settings: parallelism, storage codec, and
temporary storage. The defaults are safe for most conversions.
runtime_settings.title: Runtime Settings

ConverterOptions.title: ConverterOptions

DataTypeEnum.description: Pixel data type of the output image; `autodetect` infers it.
DataTypeEnum.title: DataTypeEnum

mode.title: Mode

mode.description: How the image is split into storage chunks.

xy_scaling.description:
Scaling factor for the XY chunk size, relative to the field of view size.

- `1`: chunks match the field of view size.
- `0.5`: chunks are half the field of view (smaller chunks, more files).
- `2`: chunks are double the field of view (larger chunks, fewer files).
xy_scaling.title: XY Scaling Factor

FovBasedChunking.title: Same as FOV

mode.title: Mode

regex.description:
Regex pattern matched against the tile's `fov_name`.

- `Include`: matching tiles are kept, all others removed.
- `Exclude`: matching tiles are removed, all others kept.
regex.title: Regex

FovNameFilter.title: FOV Name Filter

mode.description:
Keep every field of view at its original stage position. Imprecise
stage positions **may produce artifacts**: where tiles overlap, the last
written tile wins.
mode.title: Mode

Names, wavelengths, and display colors of the channels. If left
empty, the channel information parsed from the raw metadata is used.
channels.title: Channels

pixel_info.description:
Override the pixel size and the Z/time spacing of the images. If left
empty, the values parsed from the raw metadata are used.
pixel_info.title: Pixel Size Information

condition_table_path.description:
Optional path to a condition table CSV file to store in the plate
metadata.
condition_table_path.title: Condition Table Path

axes.description:
Axes of the image data, e.g. `czyx`. If left empty, the axes parsed
from the raw metadata are used.

data_type.description:
Pixel data type of the output image. `autodetect` infers it from the
input images.
data_type.title: Data Type

stage_orientation.description:
Corrections for the orientation of the microscope stage relative to
the image axes.
stage_orientation.title: Stage Orientation

filters.description: Filters selecting which tiles of the acquisition are converted.
filters.title: Filters

position_scale.description:
Scale factor (m/px) overriding ``lif_image.scale_n[10]``. Set when stage
coordinates need a non-default unit conversion.

LifAcquisitionOptions.title: LifAcquisitionOptions

LifImageAcquisitionModel.description: Acquisition input model for LIF image conversion.

path.description: Path to the acquisition LIF file or directory containing the LIF file(s).
path.title: Path

plate_name.description:
Optional custom name for the plate. If not provided, the name will be the
acquisition directory name.
plate_name.title: Plate Name

acquisition_id.description: Acquisition ID, used to identify the acquisition in case of multiple acquisitions.
acquisition_id.title: Acquisition Id

tile_scan_name.description:
Optional name of the tile scan. If ``None``, all scans in the LIF file are
processed (wildcard mode).
tile_scan_name.title: Tile Scan Name

zarr_name.description:
Optional zarr name. ``None`` derives the name as ``{lif_stem}_{scan_name}``.
Cannot be used in wildcard mode (when ``tile_scan_name`` is None).
zarr_name.title: Zarr Name

LifImageAcquisitionModel.title: LifImageAcquisitionModel

MosaicGrouping.description: Aggregate all fields of view of an acquisition into one mosaic OME-Zarr.

mode.description: How fields of view are grouped into output images.
mode.title: Mode

tiling_strategy.description:
How the fields of view are arranged within the mosaic.

- `Auto`: Automatically determine if `Snap to Grid` is possible,
  otherwise use `Snap to Corners`. Accepts an optional tolerance
  (in pixels) for grid alignment.
- `Snap to Grid`: Tile images to fit a regular grid. This is only
  possible if image positions align to a grid (potentially with
  overlap). Accepts an optional tolerance (in pixels).
- `Snap to Corners`: Tile images to fit a grid defined by the corner
  positions.
- `Inplace`: Write tiles in their original positions without tiling.
  This may lead to artifacts if microscope stage positions are not
  precise.
tiling_strategy.title: Tiling Strategy

MosaicGrouping.title: Mosaic

writer_mode.description:
Mode for writing data during conversion.

- `By Tile`: Write data one tile at a time. This consumes less memory, but may be
  slower.
- `By Tile (Using Dask)`: Write tiles in parallel using Dask. This is
  usually faster than writing by tile sequentially, but may consume more
  memory.
- `By FOV`: Write data one field of view at a time. Often the best compromise
  between speed and memory usage in most cases.
- `By FOV (Using Dask)`: Write fields of view in parallel using Dask.
  This is usually faster than writing by FOV sequentially, but may consume
  more memory.
- `In Memory`: Load all data into memory before writing.
writer_mode.title: Writer Mode

grouping.description:
How fields of view are grouped into output images.

- `Mosaic`: Aggregate all fields of view of an acquisition into one OME-Zarr,
  arranged by the nested `tiling_strategy`.
- `Per-FOV`: Write each field of view as its own OME-Zarr image (no mosaic,
  no tiling strategy).
grouping.title: Grouping

Advanced performance settings: parallelism, storage codec, and
temporary storage. The defaults are safe for most conversions.
runtime_settings.title: Runtime Settings

DefaultScheduler.title: Default

FixedSizeChunking.description: Store the image in chunks of a fixed size.

mode.description: How the image is split into storage chunks.
mode.title: Mode

FixedSizeChunking.title: Fixed Size

FovBasedChunking.description: Store the image in chunks sized like the fields of view.

mode.description: How the image is split into storage chunks.
mode.title: Mode

xy_scaling.description:
Scaling factor for the XY chunk size, relative to the field of view size.

- `1`: chunks match the field of view size.
- `0.5`: chunks are half the field of view (smaller chunks, more files).
- `2`: chunks are double the field of view (larger chunks, fewer files).
xy_scaling.title: XY Scaling Factor

FovBasedChunking.title: Same as FOV

InplaceTiling.description: Keep every field of view at its original stage position.

mode.description:
Keep every field of view at its original stage position. Imprecise
stage positions **may produce artifacts**: where tiles overlap, the last
written tile wins.
mode.title: Mode

InplaceTiling.title: Inplace

MosaicGrouping.description: Aggregate all fields of view of an acquisition into one mosaic OME-Zarr.

mode.description: How fields of view are grouped into output images.
mode.title: Mode

tiling_strategy.description:
How the fields of view are arranged within the mosaic.

- `Auto`: Automatically determine if `Snap to Grid` is possible,
  otherwise use `Snap to Corners`. Accepts an optional tolerance
  (in pixels) for grid alignment.
- `Snap to Grid`: Tile images to fit a regular grid. This is only
  possible if image positions align to a grid (potentially with
  overlap). Accepts an optional tolerance (in pixels).
- `Snap to Corners`: Tile images to fit a grid defined by the corner
  positions.
- `Inplace`: Write tiles in their original positions without tiling.
  This may lead to artifacts if microscope stage positions are not
  precise.
tiling_strategy.title: Tiling Strategy

MosaicGrouping.title: Mosaic

NamedLevels.description: Name each resolution level explicitly, from highest to lowest resolution.

mode.description: How the resolution levels of the pyramid are defined.
mode.title: Mode

level_names.description:
Names of the resolution levels, from highest to lowest resolution,
e.g. `["s0", "s1", "s2"]`. Each name becomes a path inside the OME-Zarr.
level_names.title: Level Names

NamedLevels.title: Custom Names

NumberOfLevels.description: Create a number of resolution levels with default names (`0`, `1`, ...).

mode.description: How the resolution levels of the pyramid are defined.
mode.title: Mode

num_levels.description: Number of resolution levels in the pyramid of the output image.
num_levels.title: Number of Resolution Levels

NumberOfLevels.title: Number of Levels

OmeZarrOptions.description: Options controlling the layout of the output OME-Zarr.

levels.description:
How many resolution levels the output pyramid has and how they are named.

- `Number of Levels`: a number of levels with the default names
  (`0`, `1`, ...).
- `Custom Names`: an explicit list of level names, e.g. `["s0", "s1"]`.
levels.title: Resolution Levels

chunks.description:
How the image is split into storage chunks: sized like the fields of
view (`Same as FOV`) or with fixed sizes (`Fixed Size`).
chunks.title: Chunking Strategy

ngff_version.description: Version of the OME-NGFF specification to target.
ngff_version.title: NGFF Version

table_backend.description:
File format used to store tables (e.g. ROI tables) inside the
OME-Zarr.
table_backend.title: Table Backend

OmeZarrOptions.title: OmeZarrOptions

OverwriteMode.description: How to handle existing output data: fail, replace, or extend it.
OverwriteMode.title: OverwriteMode

PerFovGrouping.description: Write each field of view as its own OME-Zarr image (no mosaic).

mode.description: How fields of view are grouped into output images.
mode.title: Mode

PerFovGrouping.title: Per-FOV

ProcessScheduler.description: Parallelize the conversion using multiple processes.

mode.description: How the conversion work is parallelized.
mode.title: Mode

num_workers.description: Number of worker processes to use. Must be at least 1.
num_workers.title: Number of Processes

ProcessScheduler.title: Processes

RuntimeSettings.description: Advanced performance settings; the defaults suit most conversions.

use_zarrs_codec.description:
Read and write image data with the `zarrs` Rust backend, which is
usually faster. **Requires** the optional `zarrs` dependency to be
installed.
use_zarrs_codec.title: Use Zarrs Codec Pipeline

dask_scheduler.description:
How the conversion work is parallelized.

- `Threads`: parallelize using multiple threads.
- `Processes`: parallelize using multiple processes.
- `Synchronous`: run sequentially, without parallelism.
- `Default`: keep the environment's parallelism settings unchanged.
dask_scheduler.title: Dask Scheduler

temp_json_options.description: Where and when intermediate conversion data is stored on disk.
temp_json_options.title: Temporary JSON Options

RuntimeSettings.title: RuntimeSettings

Scalings.description: Scaling factor applied to the field of view size to get the chunk size.
Scalings.title: Scalings

SnapToCornersTiling.description: Arrange the fields of view on a grid defined by their corners.

mode.description:
Arrange the fields of view on a grid defined by their corner
positions.
mode.title: Mode

SnapToCornersTiling.title: Snap to Corners

SnapToGridTiling.description: Arrange the fields of view on a regular grid.

mode.description:
Arrange the fields of view on a regular grid. Only possible when
their positions already align to a grid (potentially with overlap).
mode.title: Mode

tolerance.description:
Maximum misalignment (in pixels) tolerated when checking whether the
field of view positions fit a regular grid.
tolerance.title: Tiling Tolerance (in pixels)

SnapToGridTiling.title: Snap to Grid

StagePositionCorrections.description: Corrections applied to the stage positions before writing the image.

remove_xy_offset.description:
Whether to shift the image so its XY origin is 0.

- `Global`: Shift all positions together so the image origin is 0.
- `Keep`: Use the stage positions as-is. **Fails** if any position is
  negative; positive positions produce empty padding at the image origin.
remove_xy_offset.title: Remove XY Offset

remove_z_offset.description:
Whether to shift the image so its Z origin is 0.

- `Global`: Shift all positions together so the Z origin is 0.
- `Per-FOV`: Shift each field of view independently to Z origin 0.
- `Keep`: Use the stage positions as-is. **Fails** if any position is
  negative; positive positions produce empty padding at the image origin.
remove_z_offset.title: Remove Z Offset

remove_t_offset.description:
Whether to shift the image so its time origin is 0.

- `Global`: Shift all positions together so the time origin is 0.
- `Keep`: Use the stage positions as-is. **Fails** if any position is
  negative; positive positions produce empty padding at the image origin.
remove_t_offset.title: Remove T Offset

remove_xy_jitter.description:
Remove small stage position inconsistencies within a field of view by
snapping its sub-tiles to a shared origin.
remove_xy_jitter.title: Remove XY Jitter

reindex_channels.description:
If enabled, only the channels actually present are converted; if
disabled, missing channels are stored as empty images.
reindex_channels.title: Reindex Channels

StagePositionCorrections.title: StagePositionCorrections

SynchronousScheduler.description: Run the conversion sequentially, without parallelism.

mode.description: How the conversion work is parallelized.
mode.title: Mode

SynchronousScheduler.title: Synchronous

TempJsonOptions.description: Where and when intermediate conversion data is stored on disk.

temp_url.description:
Where intermediate JSON files are written. The `{zarr_dir}`
placeholder is replaced by the task's output directory.
temp_url.title: Temporary Storage URL

serialization.description:
Serialization mode for tiled image data between init and compute phases.

- `Memory`: always keep data in-memory (skips all filesystem I/O).
- `JSON`: always write to a temporary JSON file on disk (**required** for
  distributed Fractal runs where init and compute execute on different
  machines).
- `Auto`: use in-memory when the total serialized payload is within
  `max_in_memory_bytes` (default 10 MiB), otherwise fall back to JSON files
  on disk.
serialization.title: Serialization

max_in_memory_bytes.description:
Maximum total size of serialized tiled image data to keep in-memory
between init and compute phases when `serialization` is `Auto`.
If the total size exceeds this threshold, data will be written to temporary
JSON files on disk instead. Default is 10 MiB.
max_in_memory_bytes.title: Max In-Memory Bytes

TempJsonOptions.title: TempJsonOptions

ThreadScheduler.description: Parallelize the conversion using multiple threads.

mode.description: How the conversion work is parallelized.
mode.title: Mode

num_workers.description: Number of worker threads to use. Must be at least 1.
num_workers.title: Number of Threads

ThreadScheduler.title: Threads

WriterMode.description: How image data is written, trading off speed against memory usage.
WriterMode.title: WriterMode
